item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is from here, and is All Rights Reserved. As such, it has been removed.

I didn't like the 'larder' part because it's scarier to leave some stuff unexplained and I think this is more interesting as being literally living graffiti, but otherwise I really liked this. It has a really cool old-school creepy pasta vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd love to see an interaction/ crosslink between this skip and SCP-085
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You’ll love scp-4666 then, torturous body gore Santa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since I’m the first comment of 2019, I’ll keep it positive for the new year. Overall SCP was solid, like a less impressive version of 4666, the Santa one if I’m correct in my numbering. I like the feel of it, especially when it moved to a child’s playground of all places. Overall this deserves a +1, but could benefit tremendously with some updates. (And yes I know this scip is like a decade old)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love the concept, and the article itself is really well-written, but I would've liked to see more testing. This is a good skip, but would be a great one if it were just fleshed-out a bit more. Still, +1 in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept behind it, it really gives that "uneasy" feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it until the cheap 'dead kids' trope got thrown in at the last bit.

Because if your story is still lacking some edge, you can always throw in a dead kid or two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I remember this SCP scaring the shit out of me a long time ago the first time I read it. Very unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im picturing a splatoon squad trying to take this thing down

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: Where did you find this street art. I'd like to go there just so that I could nope the fuck out and nerd out lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding everyone saying that this is a good Keter because it's just damned hard to contain.
The Foundation's experiments have me a bit confused, though. The article mentions that any person who is close to SCP-1155 and not in direct line of sight of another person gets attacked.
But what about indirect observation? To me, it seems like a pretty safe bet that the Foundation would rather use a surveillance camera and a D-class rather than two D-class.
It's understandable that they would rather not do any further experiments as that immediately leads to a containment breach, but the original experiment seems rather wasteful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favourite SCP. It may sound insane, but I really want the owl thing as a tattoo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is a good question, and it does appear to be unanswered
The article said they were incoherent, and couldn't describe the occurence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really wierd. This is a piece of graffiti that is an attention seeker then abducts them, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anyone can feel free to correct me about this, but the "game" of which you speak is, I believe, a project entirely independent from the, er, foundation of the Foundation. That is, it wasn't made by the makers of the Foundation, not was it truly licensed by them, either. Bottom line, talk of it doesn't really belong here, at least in my humble opinion. That's not my call, of course, but it bothered me enough that I'm posting a comment that has nothing to do with the scip linked to this discussion page, so…

Since I probably shouldn't do that…This scip is…well, it's definitely a thing. Like others before me, I appreciate the author's demonstration of a thorough understanding of SCP object classes. What I don't get is, does the entity actually come out of the wall and attack, or do the injuries appear with no apparent source? I know outside viewers can't see, but if you've managed to interview a survivor, that seems like a pretty basic thing to ask.

Okay, I think that's sufficient justification. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its very good, i think this should be in-game, although it would be a bit odd seeing it in a laboratory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this one, it kills you doesn't bother me as it has an apparently clear reason though there is still some mystery (really appreciate how you managed that particular balancing act). Additionally very well written and isn't littered with unnecessary expunging so bravo and an emphatic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It reappears elsewhere

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure larder isn't colloquial anymore than lavatory would be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I'm confused. The description states that after it attacks someone both it and the person will disappear and it will relocate. How does it even cause more than one death(as the playground incident describes)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty much exactly why I upvoted this, plus decent writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pure nightmares.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hey, mustaches are nothing to sneeze at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: N+1thd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never heard of that legend, though it is pretty cool. Possibly the artist has… I found the picture randomly on a blog about street art. On closer inspection, found a blog with a link to a documentary/interview with the artist.

http://streetartlocator.com/blog/2009/04/03/gaias-predator-conversations-short-documentary/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's the origin of the picture? Is the picture and/or SCP based on any kind of mythology/legend in particular?

I ask because this vaguely reminds me of a creature for Native American folklore called a "stigini" - described as either an owl-like death spirit or an evil shaman that vomits up his guts in order to transform into a murderous owl-creature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. You could do away with a few words describing the SCP, but other than that, magnificent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but it becomes so much more silly when you notice that the entity has a moustache.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very cool. Good set of limitations on how it works, nicely scary picture that isn't too scary. This is a good Keter, and I appreciate good Keters, because they're so easy to do poorly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nthed. What these guys said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the thing hunts on other planets or in other dimensions, it should disappear from Earth for long, irregular intervals of time.

Or maybe there's more than one of it and they share the same pantry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. I like everything about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's a Keter that doesn't necessarily kill everyone, and it's good someone took a different tact with that classification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding. About time we had a Keter that didn't destroy everything in sight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who lives in a city with some pretty weird graffiti, this would scare the shit out of me if I went tagging with my boys and suddenly bloody murder mess. A couple tips:

  • Break up the containment procedures section, especially where there is strikethrough, so it doesn't look like a mess.
  • We use metric here, change feet, miles, inches etc…

sinewy forelimbs… predatory stance

I understand what you're trying to do here, but those words don't fit well into the clinical tone. We have a picture of this thing; you don't need to give us frilly descriptors for us to picture the object.

a description of a "larder"

Okay, this is a personal thing of mine, but I had to look up what a larder is in order to understand the apparently colloquial break in tone. It's not a big deal here because the source of the quote is mentioned, but putting quotes around a word does not excuse street language or turns of phrase that would otherwise break tone.

This is a very well-written article with no bad expungement, lolFoundation, or major breaks in tone/writing style. Congratulations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It murders everyone within a 1200 mile radius and then possesses a world leader with access to nuclear warheads. Why we don't destroy things we don't understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We haven't had a good "what kills people" in a while, let it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like its ability to teleport. I think it would be more interesting if it had to move along its surface to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This must be a major threat to reality as we know it because it craves attention and can't be concealed, making it a real headache to contain. Birds do seem to be programmed to start with the eyes, I believe, so this isn't too much of a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. Hunter, very difficult to contain, looks like an owlbear. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhh… it just reads like it's the forbidden love child of 173 and Cassy to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how this is Keter not because it's a major threat to secrecy, reality, civilization, etc., but because it's just straight-up really difficult to contain and annoying when it gets out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not in love with the core concept here, as in "monster that eats you". However, the execution is very, very good here, and my suspension of disbelief was maintained throughout. The picture was nice, and the note at the end gives it a nice creepypasta-ish finish without being stupid.

I have two quibbles: I don't know that this thing is Keter; it's very difficult to contain and kills people, but the danger it represents isn't threatening on a macro-level, beyond the prospect of any SCP that becomes publicly known if it breaches containment. Also, the skip attacks in a manner "consistent with a wild animal" or some such, but then is described as very methodically butchering its prey in a specific manner. This is internally inconsistent in my view.

Nevertheless, deftly done and well written. This has my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first paragraph of the containment procedures can be split in two, somewhere after "should be notified immediately." part.

in situations where this cannot be affected in a timely fashion

I am not sure that this usage of the word "affected" is a correct one.

The paragraph explaining how and when the entity relocates should be in the description, not in containment procedures. Specifically, everything from "Approximately every 2-4 months," and to "prevent visual contact.". "SCP-1155 cannot be permanently contained by any known means." could be removed altogether, because 1. it is obvious from the rest of the article and 2. while it might be true, the Foundation is too arrogant to openly admit its own failure. They will keep trying to find a way until they found one, and if there isn't one they will keep trying forever.

which included humanoid forms of unknown species

This is a plot hole. If the thing hunts on other planets or in other dimensions, it should disappear from Earth for long, irregular intervals of time. Or you could just remove that part, it isn't expanded into anything interesting anyway.

Also, less to this article and more to everyone who writes about other sapient species: not every sapient extraterrestrial/extradimensional liveform has to be humanoid. Right now, the ratio of humanoid sapient species to non-humanoid on the site is something like 50 to 1.

The incident logs should either be each labeled as an addendum, or the current "Addendum:" headline be renamed into "Addenda:"

I am upvoting this mostly for interesting territorial behavior the entity shows in the first incident log. The nest and the way it becomes obsessed with the ones it tasted give it enough of an unique personality to differentiate it from other "X that kills you by Y".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you hit it with paint thinner or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its/it's mixup corrected. Well spotted, sir.

Any specific advice about adjusting the tone? I've been trying to nail the clinical detachment down, but it's definitely no 450. Plus I have a tendency to be excessively verbose, and always admire the way that some of the better articles can paint a detailed picture with a few broad strokes.

Similarities to 106 are trickier. Did my best to be original, but they're both fundamentally predatory intangible creatures focused on body horror and the difficulty of containment. I guess switching it to being any graffiti rather than the picture I found would be a way to further differentiate them. I'll mull it over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I edit it to amplify the ambiguity of whether or not the victims D-89786 saw were all dead? Obviously most of the people that are partially disemboweled and then "rescued" are probably toast, but it may or may not be just eating all the people/things that it attacks, at least not immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is kind of meh and reminiscent of 106. I did like the detail about being able to see with the removed eyes. The tone could use work, and its/it's confusion almost caused me to downvote on the spot.

If you wanted to make it more interesting, I would suggest that it's some manner of malevolent consciousness that acts through graffiti rather than being a graffiti monster.

edit: Also, Imperial units towards the beginning = D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anqxyr helped me a lot with the tone and phrasing, and is the reason this doesn't suck a whole lot more.

May edit in a different picture later, I have a friend that offered to edit it to be creepier and less well-composed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still have a problem that it just kills people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, and was glad no AWCY reference appeared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, it could use a bit of polish but otherwise I enjoyed it.
